sections.paragraph:
m. and נְקָמָה, with suff. נִקְמָתִי, plur. נְקָמֹות f.-

(1) vengeance, revenge, Deu 32:35. Followed by a נִקְמֵת יְיָ the vengeance which Jehovah takes, Jer 50:15 elsewhere objectively, as נִקְמַת דָּם vengeance for slaughter, Psa 79:10 Jer 50:28, הֵיכָלֹו נִקְמַת “vengeance for his temple;” compare Jer 51:36. To take vengeance on any one is הֵשִׁיב נָקָם לְ Deu 32:41, 43 Deu 32:43 לָקַח נ׳ Isa 47:3 followed by מִן Jer 20:10 עָשָׂה נְקָמֹות בְּ Psa 149:7 Eze 25:17 followed by אֵת Mic 5:14 נָתַן נִקְמָתֹו בְּ Eze 25:14 Num 31:3. On the other hand נָתַן נְקָמֹת לְ Psa 18:48 and מִן, עָשָׂה נְקָמֹות לִפְ׳ Jud. 11:36 to give or do vengeance for any one, so as to satisfy him.

(2) the desire of vengeance, Lam 3:60. עָשָׂה בִנְקָמָה to act with the desire of vengeance, Eze 25:15.
